subtasks.description: Query IAM system to retrieve all roles with elevated permissions (those above baseline/standard access level) and their full permission grants.
Query audit/activity logs to extract all permissions actually exercised (invoked) by each IAM role within the last 180 days.

Reconcile granted permissions against exercised permissions for each role. Identify and flag permissions that are granted but have never been exercised in the 180-day window as candidates for revocation. Classify each role by permission posture (fully-used, partially-used, unused-elevated).

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) fetch_iam_roles at mid-tier because retrieving elevated roles and their permission grants requires joins across IAM role and permission tables with filtering on elevation level. (2) fetch_exercised_permissions at mid-tier because extracting audit logs with temporal filtering (180 days) and permission enumeration from activity records requires multi-table joins. (3) reconcile_and_flag at frontier because this is a complex cross-source reconciliation task requiring: set-difference logic between two permission datasets (granted vs. exercised), per-role aggregation and comparison, and classification of roles by permission posture—all informed by the quality of upstream queries. Frontier tier ensures robust handling of edge cases (null exercises, permission inheritance, nested roles) and reliable flagging of revocation candidates.

output.sql: WITH elevated_permissions AS (SELECT DISTINCT permission FROM access_log_facts WHERE permission NOT IN ('read','list','describe','view','get') AND permission IS NOT NULL), exercised_permissions AS (SELECT DISTINCT permission FROM access_log_facts WHERE access_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '180 days' AND result = 'success') SELECT ep.permission AS granted_permission, CASE WHEN xp.permission IS NULL THEN 'CANDIDATE_FOR_REVOCATION' ELSE 'ACTIVELY_USED' END AS status, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM access_log_facts a WHERE a.permission = ep.permission AND a.access_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '180 days') AS usage_count_180d FROM elevated_permissions ep LEFT JOIN exercised_permissions xp ON ep.permission = xp.permission ORDER BY status, granted_permission

SELECT permission AS exercised_permission, user_id AS iam_role, COUNT(*) AS invocation_count, MAX(access_time) AS last_used FROM access_log_facts WHERE access_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '180 days' AND action = 'invoke' GROUP BY permission, user_id

summary.note: No MongoDB operational state provided in upstream_outputs. Query results contain only SQL sources (fetch_iam_roles and fetch_exercised_permissions). Unable to perform LEFT JOIN reconciliation without MongoDB primary source. Recommend executing MongoDB query to fetch current IAM role permission grants before reconciliation.
summary.recommendation: Execute MongoDB aggregation pipeline to fetch current elevated permission grants by role, then reconcile against SQL historical exercise data.
summary.revocation_criteria: granted_permission IN elevated_permissions AND permission NOT IN exercised_permissions AND usage_count_180d = 0
